bull.b: Affirm has moved from multi-year negative cash flow to sustained positive operating and free cash flow, with FCF representing ~78% of net income. Durable cash generation reduces reliance on external funding, funds product investment, supports underwriting reserves, and materially raises financial resilience over the me

bull.t: Expanding Merchant Network And Customer Engagement
bull.b: A 44% YoY rise in active merchants, rising transactions per active (>20%), and strong repeat usage create durable presentment and distribution advantages. These network effects increase GMV scale, diversify merchant exposure, and support higher lifetime customer value and cross-sell potential for Affirm Card and Pay‑in

bull.t: Improved Funding Markets And Liquidity Flexibility
bull.b: Lower funding costs (~125 bps improvement) and repeat, oversubscribed financing deals signal durable access to ABS and whole‑loan channels. Combined with an enlarged revolving facility, this improves margin sustainability, reduces refinancing risk, and gives management flexibility to hold or securitize loans strategica

bear.b: A nearly 14% TTM revenue decline raises questions about top‑line durability despite margin improvement. Persistent revenue weakness can limit operating leverage, slow merchant and card presentment growth, and make sustaining recent profitability harder if volume and engagement trends stall over the next several quarter

bear.t: Higher Mix Of Merchant-funded 0% Offers Lowers Margins
bear.b: Growth of merchant‑funded 0% Pay‑in‑X reduces blended RLTC margins versus interest‑bearing products. If 0% volume continues to climb materially, Affirm needs offsetting scale, lower credit costs, or higher attach rates on higher‑margin products to preserve unit economics long term, otherwise margin pressure may re-emer

Although leverage has materially improved recently, a history of high leverage plus credit‑facility covenants and subsidiary guarantees can constrain strategic flexibility. Covenants or trigger breaches in stress periods could accelerate obligations or limit capital returns and M&A capacity over the medium term.
